violations.text: Raw chicken was observed stored over raw beef in the walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was properly washed/rinsed/sanitized at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. At the time of inspection, the tubing from the sanitizer bottle to the wall mounted dispenser unit was not connecting properly. Call was made for service at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Chicken, Tomato Sauce, and Peppers/Onions were observed held at 80, 82, and 78 degrees Fahrenheit respectively. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Based on time and temperature, food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Several food items portioned/prepared in the establishment were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
